input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <!-- Pull-to-refresh via RefreshView wrapper -->
        <CollectionView.Parent>
            <!-- See note below -->
        </CollectionView.Parent>

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

Since `CollectionView` doesn't have built-in pull-to-refresh, wrap it in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    private Item? _selectedItem;
    public Item? SelectedItem
    {
        get => _selectedItem;
        set
        {
            if (SetProperty(ref _selectedItem, value) && value is not null)
            {
                // Navigate, then clear selection so the user can re-select
                NavigateToDetail(value);
                SelectedItem = null;
            }
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;           // stops the spinner
    }

    private async void NavigateToDetail(Item item)
    {
        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = item });
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | Allows exactly one item selected at a time |
| `SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"` | Two-way; setter triggers navigation then resets to `null` |
| `Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"` | `RefreshView` calls this on pull-down (generated by `[RelayCommand]`) |
| `IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"` | Set to `false` after loading to dismiss the spinner |

**Don't forget** to register the route in `AppShell.xaml.cs`:
```csharp
Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(DetailPage), typeof(DetailPage));
```

And accept the parameter in `DetailViewModel` with `[QueryProperty("Item", "Item")]`.
